codeReview.summary: The revision-based cache invalidation keeps the two hot endpoint caches aligned for the runtime-managed refresh path, but the new coherence mechanism is incomplete because the non-runtime startup path never advances the shared revision, leaving stale cached responses reusable after direct runtime-state policy changes.

issues.description: The no-runtime startup path still sets `marketDataRuntimeState.initialSyncCompleted = true` directly without advancing `hotDataRevision`. Because `/simple/price` and `/coins/markets` now only invalidate on revision mismatch, any cached responses created before this direct state transition would remain valid for up to the TTL and can contradict the new post-sync policy. The shared invalidation contract should be driven from every state transition that changes hot-endpoint visibility, not only from `createMarketRuntime()`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The repo now has a cross-endpoint cache coherence invariant centered on `MarketDataRuntimeState.hotDataRevision`, but `.factory/library/runtime.md` still documents only `initialSyncCompleted`, `allowStaleLiveService`, and `syncFailureReason` as runtime state fields.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/services/market-runtime-state.ts:1-15 adds `hotDataRevision`; src/modules/simple.ts:148-186 and src/modules/coins.ts:1069-1106 depend on it for cache reuse; .factory/library/runtime.md omits the field.

summary: Reviewed handoff, commit c36b901 diff, transcript skeleton, and targeted tests. Found one blocking gap: the alternate startup path in `src/app.ts` does not bump the new shared hot-data revision, so cache coherence is not guaranteed for all runtime-state transitions that affect the hot endpoints.
